summary: PR 2596 splits the old Kirby SpecialNNs slice into a newly linked/renamed `ftkirbyspecialness.c` matching object and a separate `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` nonmatching object, adjusts split and symbol ownership metadata, moves a large prototype block out of `ftKb_Init.h` into `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h`, and updates includes and dependent item files accordingly.

key_files.role: Adjusted TU ranges: removed `ftKb_SpecialNNs.c`, added `ftkirbyspecialness.c` and `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c`, and adjusted neighboring Kirby split ranges.
Changed several Kirby float/data symbols from global names to local labels or local float metadata.
Replaced one NonMatching `ftKb_SpecialNNs.c` object with Matching `ftkirbyspecialness.c` and NonMatching `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` objects.
New Kirby Ness-special source file, added as the linked/matching object for text range 0x800FED38-0x800FF8EC.
Kirby Donkey-special source kept as a separate nonmatching object after extracting Ness-special code.
New owning header for the large Kirby Donkey/Purin/Zelda/etc. prototype block removed from `ftKb_Init.h`.
Removed 378 lines of prototypes beginning after Kirby Ness-special declarations.
Includes the new `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h` header and removes two `Vec4` data definitions from this TU.
Include surface updated to reference `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h` and retain access to `ftKb_Init.h` via explicit Kirby include.
Include cleanup and dependency update toward the new `ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h` header.
Neighboring Kirby special TU had text/sdata2 split boundaries adjusted.
Small dependent motion-state update associated with the split/rename.
Dependent item include/reference update for Kirby copied special interactions.
Dependent item include/reference update for Kirby copied special interactions.
Dependent item include/reference update for Kirby copied special interactions.
Dependent item include/reference update for Kirby copied special interactions.
Dependent item include/reference update for Kirby copied special interactions.
Dependent item include/reference update for Kirby copied special interactions.

what_changed: Renamed/replaced the old `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNNs.c` object entry with `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialness.c` in `splits.txt` and `configure.py`.
Marked `ftkirbyspecialness.c` as `Object(Matching, ...)` and introduced `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` as a separate `Object(NonMatching, ...)` in `configure.py`.
Split text ownership in `splits.txt`: `ftkirbyspecialness.c` covers 0x800FED38-0x800FF8EC and `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` covers 0x800FF8EC-0x80105BA8.
Adjusted neighboring Kirby special ranges, including shortening `ftkirbyspecialfox.c` text end from 0x800FEDD0 to 0x800FED38 and extending its sdata2 end to 0x804D9478.
Moved data ownership for `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` to `.data` 0x803CB720-0x803CB730 and changed `ftKb_Init_803CB720` symbol metadata from global to local float data.
Changed `ftKb_Init_804D9478`, `ftKb_Init_804D947C`, and `ftKb_Init_804D9480` from named globals to local sdata2 float labels `@228`, `@229`, and `@237`.
Removed a large prototype block from `ftKb_Init.h` and added a new `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h` with the corresponding declarations.
Updated Kirby and item source includes to use the new header boundaries after the split.

smart_moves.text: Separated the newly linked Ness-special code from the still-nonmatching Donkey-special range instead of keeping one broad nonmatching `ftKb_SpecialNNs.c` TU.
Kept matching status narrow: only `ftkirbyspecialness.c` is marked Matching while `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` remains NonMatching.
Updated split and symbol metadata together with the source/header rename, reducing mismatch between source organization and config ownership.
Moved declarations out of an overloaded init header into a more specific Kirby special header surface.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a large nonmatching fighter TU contains a newly matchable subrange, split the object by real text boundaries and mark only the verified subrange as Matching while leaving the rest NonMatching.

Header cleanup should follow TU ownership: after extracting a specific special-move file, move its declarations to the more specific header and update dependent includes rather than leaving all prototypes in an init header.
Split metadata and symbol scope changes can be part of a focused source rename/link PR when they directly describe the new TU boundaries and local data ownership.

naming_conventions.convention: Kirby copied special source filenames use lower-case `ftkirbyspecial<character>.c` style here, e.g. `ftkirbyspecialness.c`, `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c`, and existing `ftkirbyspecialfox.c`.

Function names for Kirby copied specials retain the `ftKb_<character-or-copy>SpecialN...` pattern visible in the moved prototype block, such as `ftKb_DkSpecialNStart_Anim` and `ftKb_SpecialNDk_800FF8EC`.
Local float/data symbols may be represented with local labels such as `@228`, `@229`, and `@237` rather than promoted `ftKb_Init_*` globals when symbol metadata indicates local ownership.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use split-boundary surgery to carve a matching subrange out of a larger nonmatching object: `ftkirbyspecialness.c` begins at 0x800FED38 and ends at 0x800FF8EC, with `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` starting at 0x800FF8EC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR evidence marks the new object Matching in `configure.py`, but no objdiff/build output or PR discussion was included in the slice.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjust adjacent TU boundaries and sdata2 ownership together when a renamed/split file changes the exact text and literal ranges.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No reviewer explanation was available for the exact section-boundary rationale.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Demote or relabel data symbols to local labels when split ownership indicates the data belongs to a local subrange rather than a globally named init symbol.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Treat as a metadata tactic from this PR, not a standalone global naming rule.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` becoming Matching.
Search for references to `ftkirbyspecialness.c` and whether additional headers were split after PR 2596.
Search for later changes to `ftKb_Init_803CB720`, `@228`, `@229`, or `@237` to validate data ownership after this split.
Search for PRs touching `ftKb_SpecialNNs.c` before PR 2596 to understand why the Ness and Donkey ranges were previously combined.
Search item files `itkirbyyoshispecialn.c`, `itkirbygamewatchchefpan.c`, `itmewtwoshadowball.c`, `itpeachtoad.c`, and `itseakneedleheld.c` for follow-up include or prototype cleanup.

notes: Changed-file metadata is complete for 18 files and the diff excerpt includes the central split/config/header evidence.
The PR body is empty and there are no issue comments, review comments, or reviews in the supplied slice.
The raw diff is truncated in loaded context, so some per-file item edits are only classified from changed-file metadata rather than exact line-level content.
No build, objdiff, checkdiff, or CI output was included; matching status is only evidenced by `configure.py` marking `ftkirbyspecialness.c` as Matching.
No reviewer intent or validation result is inferred.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Split matching subranges without overclaiming the rest of a TU
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2596 supports a narrow lesson: when extracting a matchable subrange from a broad nonmatching fighter TU, update split ranges and build config so only the verified/newly linked file is marked Matching while the remaining range stays NonMatching.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Kirby copied special filenames use lower-case character suffixes in this area
accepted_candidate_records.text: This PR replaces `ftKb_SpecialNNs.c` with `ftkirbyspecialness.c` and adds `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c`, matching the existing lower-case `ftkirbyspecialfox.c` filename style in the neighboring split entries.

source_update_candidates.title: ftkirbyspecialness.c split and matching status in PR 2596
source_update_candidates.text: `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialness.c` was introduced for the 0x800FED38-0x800FF8EC text range and marked `Object(Matching, ...)` in `configure.py` by PR 2596.
source_update_candidates.reason: Path/split status is source-owned metadata and should be curated as a path fact, not promoted directly by intake.

source_update_candidates.title: ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c split and nonmatching status in PR 2596
source_update_candidates.text: `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` was split out for the 0x800FF8EC-0x80105BA8 text range with `.data` 0x803CB720-0x803CB730 and kept as `Object(NonMatching, ...)` in `configure.py` by PR 2596.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a concrete path/split status candidate needing curator confirmation against current source.

source_update_candidates.title: ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h owns declarations moved out of ftKb_Init.h in PR 2596
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2596 removes a large block of Kirby copied-special prototypes from `ftKb_Init.h` and adds `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h`, with sources updated to include the new header.
source_update_candidates.reason: Header ownership facts should be checked against current headers before source-corpus update.

source_update_candidates.title: Narrow matching status when splitting a nonmatching object
source_update_candidates.text: Potential standard refinement: when splitting a nonmatching TU to link one subrange, update config so only the proven subrange is Matching and leave the rest NonMatching; include split boundary and verification evidence when available.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR demonstrates the pattern, but the slice lacks validation output; curator should decide whether this belongs in standards or remains a local lesson.

source_update_candidates.title: Kirby special local float symbol metadata changed in PR 2596
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2596 changes `ftKb_Init_803CB720` to local float data and relabels sdata2 floats at 0x804D9478, 0x804D947C, and 0x804D9480 as local labels `@228`, `@229`, and `@237`.
source_update_candidates.reason: Data/symbol ownership should be curated in the appropriate data or path source, not treated as a global naming standard from intake alone.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all functions in `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` matched; `configure.py` explicitly keeps that object NonMatching.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale or validation details; the slice has no comments, reviews, or build/objdiff logs.
Do not promote local labels like `@228` as a universal naming preference; this PR only evidences specific symbol metadata edits.
Do not treat the PR title alone as proof of exact semantic ownership beyond the split/config/header changes visible in the diff.
